lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads 23:00. We're {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
[pause] I want you to find somewhere soft to sit, if you're not already. A chair. A bed. The floor is fine.
Close your eyes. Not yet — in a moment. First, I want you to notice the sound of your own breathing.
[pause] You're already doing it. You've been breathing all night. You'll keep breathing.
Now close your eyes.
[pause] Breathe in through your nose. Four count. One. Two. Three. Four.
Hold. One. Two. Three. Four.
Release through your mouth. Slow. [pause] There.
Again. In. Hold. Release.
[pause] Your shoulders are tight. I can hear it in the silence. Let them drop.
The people outside are not your responsibility. Say that to yourself. [pause] Again.
The people outside are not your responsibility.
[pause] Scan your body now. Start at the crown of your head. Is there tension there?
Move down. Your face. Your jaw.
I hear a lot of people are clenching their jaws tonight. You don't have to.
[pause] Your neck. Your shoulders. Your chest — where you hold the fear.
Your hands. Unclench them. There's nothing to grip right now.
[pause] Your belly. Your thighs. Your feet.
All of it is still here. All of it is still yours.
[pause] The numbers are heavier this year. I know. You know. Everyone listening knows.
The count is up. {{ELAPSED}} in and the count is already steep.
[pause] But you are not the count. You are not the number. You are the one who said no.
You chose the door. You chose the lock. You chose to stay.
[pause] Picture that choice. See it clearly. You made it hours ago.
You could have walked out at 19:01. You didn't.
[pause] Breathe into that. In. Hold. Release.
Now picture the people you have decided not to forgive. [pause] Don't push them away. Just... look at them.
The ones who said you were weak for hiding. The ones who asked why you didn't fight.
[pause] They are not in this room with you right now.
They are somewhere else, making different choices. Ka-shuh-lem-na-tay.
[pause] You don't have to carry their judgment. You can set it down.
Breathe. In. Hold. Release.
[pause] I'm going to tell you about silence now.
Silence is not absence. Silence is a choice you made.
[pause] The sirens you're not hearing — that's because you closed the window. That's because you turned down the radio before 19:00.
The screams you're not hearing — they're not your business.
[pause] I know that sounds harsh. It's meant to be kind.
You cannot save people who are not in your house. You cannot save yourself if you open the door.
[pause] So the silence is protection. The silence is love.
Breathe into it. In. Hold. Release.
[pause] There are 4 more hours until midnight passes. There are 8 more hours until the light.
You will survive both. You have already survived the hard part — the first hours, when it was new.
[pause] Now you're tired. That's good. Tiredness means time is moving.
Tiredness means you're going to make it.
[pause] Open your eyes whenever you're ready.
The clock reads 23:00 still, or very close. We're still here. You're still safe.
[pause] I'll be back at 03:00. For now, breathe.
And remember: the ones outside are not your responsibility.
[pause] You chose the door. You made the right choice.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide: 61 confirmed as of 23:00. East Side cluster +18% from this point last year.
Willy Street barricade holding steady. State Street corridor: activity concentrated south of University.
"The silence is protection. The silence is love."
PurgeShield panels: installation callbacks available through dawn. Check your seals. Every gap is a choice.
